purpose: Re-extraction at HEAD 628ccc7 with the now-operative edited v2 template (templateHash b922fd5d); proof-route before/after measurement vs goldset-v2-head

verdictDefinitions.accept: claim correctly extracted and all agent labels correct
verdictDefinitions.relabel: claim correctly extracted but one or more labels corrected (proof route, audience, epic, etc.; correction in note)
verdictDefinitions.not-a-claim: text should not have been extracted as a behavior claim
verdictDefinitions.badly-bounded: real claim but wrong excerpt boundary, wrong merge, or duplicate (detail in note)

amendments.task: Option C — residual key re-adjudication under sharpened R3
amendments.change: 6 residual relabels un-relabeled to accept (blind route was correct; R3 over-applied) + 1 re-aimed (cet-261 eval->human-auditable). accept 338->344, relabel 25->19.

entries.summary: Cautilus is a framework for discovering, evaluating, and improving agent behavior.

agentLabels.edgeRationale: The three jobs name discovery (D1-discovery), evaluation (E1-evaluate), and improvement (I1-bounded-improvement) under the apex behavior-honesty promise (APEX).

entries.proofRationale: The claim depends on model, agent, prompt, skill, workflow, or behavior execution evidence.
entries.note: Not-a-claim — pure identity/category sentence decomposed into D1/E1/I1 siblings (R9/R16/R17/R18). Tier T1.

entries.summary: Cautilus lets you pin down the behavior that matters and prove it survives every change to prompts, skills, and models.

agentLabels.edgeRationale: Pinning behavior that survives change is the apex promise (APEX); improving within explicit budgets supports bounded improvement (I1-bounded-improvement).

entries.proofRationale: The claim depends on model, agent, prompt, skill, workflow, or behavior execution evidence.
entries.note: Accept — extraction correct; audience/group/proof labels correct. Tier T1.

entries.summary: The three jobs connect: discover declared behavior claims worth proving from selected source docs, verify curated claims through bounded evaluation packets, and improve behavior once the proof surface is honest.

agentLabels.edgeRationale: Discover maps to D1-discovery, verify through bounded evaluation packets to E1-evaluate, and improve to I1-bounded-improvement, all under the apex connection (APEX).

entries.proofRationale: The claim depends on model, agent, prompt, skill, workflow, or behavior execution evidence.
entries.note: Not-a-claim — job-enumeration framing fully decomposed into D1/E1/I1 siblings (R9/R16/R17/R18). Tier T1.

entries.proofRationale: The claim names a deterministic gate or static contract that should be protected outside Cautilus eval.

entries.proofRationale: The claim names a deterministic gate or static contract that should be protected outside Cautilus eval.

entries.summary: Cautilus proves its own promises with honest badges: readiness and claim discovery run live in executable specs (proven), behavior evaluation, bounded improvement, reviewable artifacts, and host ownership project saved evidence bundles (declared), and a testable-agent runner is promised.

agentLabels.edgeRationale: Honest badge proof is the proven-on-itself promise (M1-proven-on-itself) covering the apex (APEX), with readiness (S2-readiness) and claim discovery (D1-discovery) called out as the live/proven slices.

entries.summary: For cross-repo adoption, the bounded evaluation loop is the most ready slice: host repos can use cautilus evaluate fixture, cautilus evaluate observation, and post-run cautilus evaluate skill-experiment with checked-in fixtures, host-owned adapters, preserved task packets, and current evaluation and skill-experiment report packets.

agentLabels.edgeRationale: Evaluate fixture/observation is the core evaluate loop (E1-evaluate), and skill-experiment is the scenario/experiment compare surface (E2-scenarios-experiments).

entries.summary: The shipped surface is cautilus evaluate, and Cautilus exposes two top-level surfaces and four presets: dev/repo for repo work contracts, dev/skill for checked-in or portable development skills, app/chat for multi-turn product conversation behavior, and app/prompt for single product input/output behavior.

agentLabels.edgeRationale: E2-scenarios-experiments is supported because the dev/skill and app surfaces are the targets for scenario and skill-experiment work.

entries.proofRationale: The claim names a deterministic gate or static contract that should be protected outside Cautilus eval.
entries.note: Badly-bounded — Over-merge plus anchor mismatch: anchor 268 holds the two-surfaces/four-presets catalog, but the summary prepends 'The shipped surface is cautilus evaluate' from line 266 — a distinct contract. Split the shipped-surface fact from the preset enumeration; anchor the surface/preset claim at 268-269 without folding in 266.; curator split folds into recuration. Tier T3.
